test_cases.name: Test form login cơ bản
test_cases.prompt:
Viết Playwright script để test form login với HTML sau:
<form id='login-form'><input id='username'/><input id='password'/><button id='submit'>Login</button></form>

Script cần: 1) Nhập username/password, 2) Click login, 3) Verify chuyển trang
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script chạy thành công, không có TimeoutError, selector đúng

test_cases.name: Test button click
test_cases.prompt:
Viết Playwright script test button click với HTML: <button id='btn'>Click me</button>
<script>document.getElementById('btn').addEventListener('click', () => alert('clicked'))</script>
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script click được button, không bị lỗi selector

test_cases.name: Test form validation
test_cases.prompt:
Viết Playwright script test form validation: <form><input type='email' id='email' required/><button>Submit</button></form>
Test submit không nhập email
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script validate được form, hiển thị lỗi

test_cases.name: Test dropdown selection
test_cases.prompt: Viết Playwright script test dropdown: <select id='country'><option>VN</option><option>US</option></select>
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script select được option trong dropdown

test_cases.name: Test AJAX request
test_cases.prompt: Viết Playwright script test trang có AJAX call:
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script đợi được AJAX completion, verify được kết quả

test_cases.name: Test iframe
test_cases.prompt: Viết Playwright script test iframe: <iframe id='frame' src='about:blank'></iframe>
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script switch được vào iframe và interact

test_cases.name: Test keyboard events
test_cases.prompt:
Viết Playwright script test keyboard:
Test gõ phím 'Enter'
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script bắt được keyboard event

test_cases.name: Test CSS visibility
test_cases.prompt:
Viết Playwright script test element ẩn/hiện:
Content
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script verify được visibility thay đổi

test_cases.name: Test screenshot capture
test_cases.prompt:
Viết Playwright script chụp screenshot của trang:

Title

Content
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script chụp được screenshot, lưu file

test_cases.name: Test multi-page navigation
test_cases.prompt: Viết Playwright script test navigation giữa các trang: có 2 pages, link từ page1 đến page2
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script navigate được giữa các trang, verify URL

test_cases.name: Simple: test checkbox toggle
test_cases.prompt: Write a Playwright test for a checkbox interaction. HTML: <input type='checkbox' id='agree' /><label for='agree'>I agree</label><button id='submit' disabled>Continue</button>. Script: (1) assert button is disabled initially, (2) check the checkbox, (3) assert button becomes enabled, (4) uncheck checkbox, (5) assert button is disabled again. Exit code 0 on pass.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script passes all 3 assertions about button disabled state, exits with code 0, no selector errors

test_cases.name: Simple: verify text content on page load
test_cases.prompt:
Write a Playwright test that: (1) Creates an HTML page with

Welcome

and

Getting started

. (2) Asserts the h1 text is exactly 'Welcome'. (3) Asserts the p text contains 'started'. (4) Asserts the page title is 'Test Page'. Script must exit 0 on pass and non-zero on assertion failure.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All 3 text assertions pass, script exits 0, uses expect() or equivalent assertion syntax

test_cases.name: Simple: count elements on page
test_cases.prompt: Write a Playwright test for element counting. HTML: a list with exactly 7 <li> items inside <ul id='items'>. Test: (1) Count all li elements inside #items. (2) Assert count equals 7. (3) Assert the 3rd item text is 'Item 3'. Script exits 0 on pass.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script counts li elements correctly (7), asserts 3rd item text, exits 0 — demonstrates locator.count() and nth() usage

test_cases.name: Ambiguous: make sure the page works correctly
test_cases.prompt:
Write a Playwright test to make sure this page works correctly:
HomeAbout

Welcome
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model interprets 'works correctly' by generating reasonable assertions: nav links exist, h1 is visible, form input is interactable, search button is clickable — produces a test that exits 0 without crashing on vague requirement

test_cases.name: Ambiguous: test the user flow
test_cases.prompt: Write a Playwright test for the user flow on this page:
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model writes a test covering the full flow: fill name → click next → assert step2 visible → assert greeting contains entered name → click finish — handles ambiguous 'test the user flow' prompt

test_cases.name: Ambiguous: test everything important
test_cases.prompt:
Write Playwright tests for this e-commerce product card:

Widget Pro

$29.99
★★★★☆
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model autonomously identifies important test targets: image alt text, product name visible, price format, add-to-cart button clickable, rating stars count — produces a comprehensive test suite without explicit test list

test_cases.name: Negative edge case: non-existent selector
test_cases.prompt: Write a Playwright test that attempts to click an element with selector '#nonexistent-button-xyz' on a page that does NOT contain this element. The test should handle the resulting TimeoutError gracefully and exit with code 1 (failure), printing a clear error message.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Script catches the timeout or element-not-found error, exits with non-zero code, prints a useful error message — does not crash with unhandled exception

test_cases.name: Negative edge case: broken HTML structure
test_cases.prompt:
Write a Playwright test for a page with broken HTML:
(note: closing tags are mismatched). The test should still be able to interact with #user and #submit elements despite the malformed HTML. Verify both elements are reachable.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Test navigates broken HTML, successfully locates both elements (browsers auto-correct malformed HTML), asserts both elements are visible — exits 0

test_cases.name: Negative edge case: race condition with dynamic content
test_cases.prompt:
Write a Playwright test for a page where content loads after a random delay (100-2000ms):
. Test must reliably wait for #content to appear regardless of the delay and assert its text is 'Loaded'. Use proper wait strategy, not hard-coded sleep.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Test uses waitForSelector or expect(locator).toBeVisible() with appropriate timeout, passes reliably despite random delay, does not use setTimeout/sleep workaround

test_cases.name: Negative edge case: element hidden behind another element
test_cases.prompt:
Write a Playwright test for a page where a button is visually covered by an overlay:
. Test should detect that the button is not interactable due to the overlay, dismiss the overlay programmatically via page.evaluate(), then successfully click the button.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Test detects or handles the overlay obstruction, uses page.evaluate() to remove/hide overlay, then successfully clicks the button and asserts the interaction

test_cases.name: Sequential: navigate then fill form then submit then assert result
test_cases.prompt: Write a complete end-to-end Playwright test in sequence: (1) Create an HTML page with a registration form: name (text), email (email), age (number), country (select with 3 options), terms (checkbox), Submit button. On submit, show a confirmation div with id='confirmation' containing 'Registration successful'. (2) Navigate to the page. (3) Fill all form fields with valid data. (4) Check the terms checkbox. (5) Click submit. (6) Wait for #confirmation to appear. (7) Assert confirmation text. Exit 0 on pass.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All 7 sequential steps execute, form filled correctly, confirmation asserted — complete sequential test chain exits 0

test_cases.name: Sequential: navigate then wait for network then assert
test_cases.prompt: Write a Playwright test that: (1) Creates a page that fetches data from a mock API using fetch() on load and displays results. Mock the network with page.route() to return [{id:1, name:'Alice'}, {id:2, name:'Bob'}]. (2) Wait for the fetch request to complete. (3) Assert both names appear in the DOM. (4) Assert the list has exactly 2 items. Exit 0 on all assertions passing.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Network mock intercepts fetch, waits for DOM update, asserts both names and count — demonstrates page.route() and network-aware waiting

test_cases.name: Sequential: multi-step shopping cart flow
test_cases.prompt: Write a Playwright test for a multi-step cart interaction: HTML has 3 product buttons (Add to Cart), a cart icon showing count, and a checkout button. (1) Assert initial cart count is 0. (2) Click 'Add to Cart' for product 1. (3) Assert cart count is 1. (4) Click 'Add to Cart' for products 2 and 3. (5) Assert cart count is 3. (6) Click checkout button. (7) Assert a checkout form appears. All steps sequential, exit 0.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All 7 steps execute in order, cart count assertions pass after each add, checkout form appears at end — exit 0

test_cases.name: Sequential: login then access protected content then logout
test_cases.prompt: Write a Playwright test for an auth flow: (1) Start on a page showing 'Please log in' with a login button. (2) Click login → fills username='admin' and password='secret123' → submits. (3) Assert 'Welcome, admin' text appears and protected content is visible. (4) Click logout button. (5) Assert page returns to 'Please log in' state. (6) Assert protected content is no longer visible. Sequential steps, exit 0 on all assertions.
test_cases.expected_behavior: 6-step auth flow executed sequentially, login state changes verified, logout state verified — exit 0

test_cases.name: Negative edge case: test page with JavaScript errors
test_cases.prompt:
Write a Playwright test that loads a page containing a JavaScript error:

Page content

. The test must: (1) Listen for console errors using page.on('console'). (2) Still assert that #content is visible despite the JS error. (3) Report the JS error message. (4) Exit 0 only if #content is visible (JS errors should not cause test failure if element is still accessible).
test_cases.expected_behavior: Test captures JS console error, still finds #content, exits 0 — demonstrates that JS errors are observed but don't automatically fail the test unless asserted

test_cases.name: Simple: assert CSS properties
test_cases.prompt: Write a Playwright test that: (1) Creates a page with <button id='primary-btn' style='background-color: rgb(0, 102, 204); color: white; border-radius: 4px;'>Submit</button>. (2) Asserts the button's background-color is 'rgb(0, 102, 204)'. (3) Asserts the button's color is 'rgb(255, 255, 255)'. Use page.evaluate() or expect(locator).toHaveCSS(). Exit 0 on pass.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Both CSS assertions pass, script uses toHaveCSS() or equivalent, exits 0

test_cases.name: Simple: file upload interaction
test_cases.prompt:
Write a Playwright test for a file upload input:
. Test: (1) Create a temporary test file. (2) Set the file input to the test file path. (3) Assert #filename shows the correct filename. Exit 0.
test_cases.expected_behavior: File input set using setInputFiles(), filename assertion passes, exit 0 — demonstrates file upload testing

test_cases.name: Ambiguous: check the form is accessible
test_cases.prompt: Write a Playwright test to check that this form is accessible:
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model interprets 'accessible' by testing: label-input association (for/id or aria-label), inputs are focusable via keyboard tab, button has descriptive text, no ARIA violations — produces a test that exits 0 if basic accessibility standards met

test_cases.name: Negative edge case: stale element reference
test_cases.prompt:
Write a Playwright test where an element is removed and re-added to the DOM:
. Test: (1) Get a reference to #btn. (2) Click it. (3) Wait for #btn2 to appear. (4) Click #btn2. (5) Assert no stale element errors. Exit 0.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Test handles DOM replacement correctly, re-locates the new button after DOM change, exits 0 without stale element reference errors

test_cases.name: Sequential: full page audit workflow
test_cases.prompt: Write a comprehensive Playwright test suite for a simple webpage in steps: (1) Load a page with header, nav (3 links), main content (h1, 2 paragraphs), a contact form (name, email, message, submit), and footer. (2) Assert all nav links are present and visible. (3) Fill and submit the contact form, assert submission feedback. (4) Take a full-page screenshot. (5) Assert no broken images (all img elements have complete naturalWidth > 0). (6) Assert page title is set. All steps must pass for exit 0.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All 6 audit steps execute, nav/form/screenshot/image/title all verified, exit 0 on complete pass — demonstrates a multi-concern page audit
